mechanism_narrative: Elevated FEDFUNDS levels increase the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically reducing present values and producing negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds its historical median over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals deteriorating aggregate demand and triggers institutional de-risking, leading to negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly elevated CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS compress real disposable income and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction is not statistically significant in a 36-month rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales during post-recession recovery signal reviving consumer demand and upward earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: In months classified as post-recession recovery, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations despite rising RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which produce positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns through higher corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1200 thousand units signal weakening construction activity and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns through downstream earnings contraction.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1200, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE signal tight labor market dynamics that drive wage growth and consumption, producing positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of PAYEMS and inverted UNRATE yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 signals expanding aggregate output and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth surprises are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of quarters over a 5-year rolling window.
